# @byted-service/timer

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/timer.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/timer) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/timer)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/timer)

[简体中文](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/timer/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/timer/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/timer/CHANGELOG.md)

Used for various asynchronous operation timing and timeout calculation.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/timer --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

## Quick start

```ts
import { promisify } from 'util';

import { Timer } from '@byted-service/timer';

const sleep = promisify(setTimeout);

(async () => {
    const timer = new Timer(1000);

    await sleep(10);

    console.log(
        'AsyncOperation took %d ms, and %d ms left.',
        timer.cost(),
        timer.remain()
    );
    console.log(
        'AsyncOperation took %d μs, and %d μs left.',
        timer.cost('μs'),
        timer.remain('μs')
    );
    console.log(
        'AsyncOperation took %d ns, and %d ns left.',
        timer.cost('ns'),
        timer.remain('ns')
    );
})();
```

Use `timer.call()` to retry asynchronous operations.

```ts
import { promisify } from 'util';

import { Timer } from '@byted-service/timer';

const sleep = promisify(setTimeout);

(async () => {
    const timer = new Timer(1000);

    const result = await timer.call(
        async (timeout, i) => {
            console.log(
                'Try asyncOperation with %d ms, %d times',
                timeout,
                i + 1
            );

            await sleep(timeout);

            if (i < 2) {
                throw new Error('Try later');
            }

            return 'async result';
        },
        { fnTimeout: 10, times: 3 }
    );

    console.log('Got result from asyncOperation: "%s"', result);

    try {
        await timer.call(
            async (timeout, i) => {
                console.log(
                    'Try asyncOperation with %d ms, %d times',
                    timeout,
                    i + 1
                );

                await sleep(timeout);

                throw new Error('Try later');
            },
            {
                fnTimeout: 10,
                times: 3,
                finalError: (err) =>
                    new Error(`Finally got error "${err.message}"`),
            }
        );
    } catch (err) {
        console.log('Got error: %s', err);
    }
})();
```

Use `timer.child()` to get multiple `timers` for timing separately.

```ts
import { promisify } from 'util';

import { Timer } from '@byted-service/timer';

const sleep = promisify(setTimeout);

(async () => {
    const timer = new Timer(1000);

    const readTimer = timer.child();
    await sleep(10);
    console.log('Read operation took %d ms', readTimer.cost());

    const writeTimer = timer.child();
    await sleep(10);
    console.log('Write operation took %d ms', writeTimer.cost());

    console.log('All operations took %d ms', timer.cost());
})();
```

Use `timer.timeline()` to record the time spent on each asynchronous operation.

```ts
import { promisify } from 'util';

import { Timer } from '@byted-service/timer';

const sleep = promisify(setTimeout);

(async () => {
    const timer = new Timer(1000);

    const middlewares = new Array(10)
        .fill('')
        .map((_, i) => ({ name: `middleware${i}`, fn: () => sleep(i) }));

    for (const middleware of middlewares) {
        timer.timeline(middleware.name);
        await middleware.fn();
        timer.timeline(middleware.name);
    }

    console.log('All middlewares took %O', timer.timelines());
})();
```

## API

### new Timer([options])

Create timer.

-   options: When it is `number | null`, it is equivalent to `options.timeout`.
    -   unit: Time unit, the default value is `ms`
    -   timeout: Timeout, the default value is `null` ( Infinity )
    -   startOnCreate: Whether to automatically call `timer.start()` upon creation, the default value is `true`

### timer.unit

Timer unit.

### timer.timeout

Timer timeout.

### timer.start()

Start timer, it can be used with `options.startOnCreate` for the case of `time to create timer != time to start timer`.

### timer.stop([unit])

Stop timer, the internal state of the timer will stop changing after the call.

-   `timer.cost()`、`timer.remain()` will return a fixed value.
-   `timer.call()`、`timer.child()`、`timer.timeline()` will throw.

### timer.cost([unit])

Query timer cost time.

-   unit: Time unit, the default value is [timer.unit](#timerunit).

### timer.remain([unit[, timeout]])

Query timer remain time.

-   unit: Time unit, the default value is [timer.unit](#timerunit).
-   timeout: Timeout, the default value is [timer.timeout](#timertimeout).

PS: If it is infinite, it will return `Infinity`.

### timer.call(fn[, options])

Try to call the function within the time limit.

-   fn: Call function.
-   options.times: The number of attempts, the default value is 1.
-   options.fnTimeout: The maximum timeout time for each call, the default value is [timer.remain()](#timerremainunit-timeout).
-   options.unit: Time unit, the default value is [timer.unit](#timerunit)
-   options.finalError: The final error of the call failure, the function return value will be thrown when it is a function, otherwise the value will be thrown directly.

PS: If the time is infinite, the first parameter of `fn` will be `undefined`.

### timer.child([options])

Get child timer.

-   options: See [new Timer()](#new-timeroptions).

### timer.hasTimeline(key)

Is there a timeline.

-   key: Timeline key.

### timer.delTimeline(key)

Delete a timeline.

-   key: Timeline key.

### timer.timeline(key[, unit])

Start or stop timeline.

-   key: Timeline key.
-   unit: Time unit.

PS: Second call means stop.

### timer.timelines([unit])

-   unit: Time unit, the default value is [timer.unit](#timerunit)
-   Returns: Timeline array.
    -   key: Timeline key.
    -   cost: Timeline cost.
    -   unit: Time unit.
    -   stoped: Whether the timeline is stopped.
